summary: Soaring Standing On The Moon, blistering Jack Straw, tight second‑set jam
blurb: The show kicks off with a high‑energy One More Saturday Night and a blistering Jack Straw that sets the tone. The second set shines with a soaring Standing On The Moon, a fiery Same Thing, and a tight rhythm section that drives the band to a triumphant Throwing Stones reprise. Vince’s keyboards add depth while Phil’s bass sits front‑and‑center throughout.
review: December 28, 1991 at the Oakland Coliseum delivered one of those rare nights where the second set eclipses everything that came before it. After an electric opener – One More Saturday Night rolling straight into a scorching Jack Straw – the band settled into a groove that only got tighter as the night progressed. Standing On The Moon soars over a sea of Phil’s “mega‑bass” and Mickey’s monster drumming, while Jerry’s guitar wails with his signature blues‑bent tone and a daring DigiTech Whammy on Foolish Heart. The Same Thing erupts into a fiery jam that many fans call “blistering,” leading seamlessly into a Throwing Stones reprise that ties the set together before a rousing U.S. Blues encore. Vince Welnick’s rollicking keys, from the piano‑driven Loser to the organ‑rich Cassidy and Deal, add lush texture throughout, and despite a few lyric slips early in the second set, Bob Weir’s rhythm work holds everything together. In short, this show captures the Dead at a peak of improvisational fire and tight ensemble play – a must‑listen for anyone wanting that classic early‑’90s magic.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Five of six recordings label the show as positive, one mixed; reviewers repeatedly praise the second‑set flow, standout songs (Jack Straw, Standing On The Moon, Same Thing), and tight rhythm section. Consistent language like “soaring,” “blistering,” and “legendary” across multiple sources justifies a strong positive sentiment boost.

best_recording.reason: High‑quality soundboard with clear mix, forward‑placed bass and toms, and no major dropouts – captures the second set brilliance perfectly.

key_highlights: Soaring Standing On The Moon paired with a fiery Same Thing in the second set.
Blistering Jack Straw opener that sets an electrified tone for the night.
Phil’s ‘mega‑bass’ front‑and‑center driving the band through tight jams.
Vince Welnick’s rollicking keyboard textures adding depth to every song.

band_performance.Jerry: Soaring vocals and blistering guitar work, especially on Jack Straw, Standing On The Moon and the fiery Same Thing; uses a blues‑bent tone and DigiTech Whammy for extra color.
band_performance.Phil: Thick, beefy ‘mega‑bass’ sits front‑and‑center, romping all night and delivering a standout Minglewood jam.
band_performance.Bob: Solid rhythm guitar and vocal contributions; early lyric slip quickly recovered, serving as the glue of the set.
band_performance.Mickey: Powerful ‘monster’ drumming that drives the band forward on Uncle John's Band, U.S. Blues and throughout the second set.
band_performance.Billy: Banged and drove the music with tight fills, complementing Mickey especially during high‑energy moments like Throwing Stones.
